input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.7.1a specified in the FSAE rule document? To answer the question, use the scale bar shown at the top of the engineering drawing to compute necessary dimensions in the drawing.  First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.7.1
            TITLE: any part of any aerodynamic device that is located:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.7.1: Any part of any Aerodynamic Device that is located:
T.7.7.1 Any part of any Aerodynamic Device that is located:   
a. In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 1200 mm above the ground  
b. Outside of the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 500 mm above the 
ground
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 71 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles and outboard of two vertical planes 
parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside of the front tires at the height 
of the hubs must be no higher than 250 mm above the ground

final_answer:
Explanation:Rule T.7.7.1a states that any part of the Aerodynamic Device located in the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 1200 mm above the ground. Based on the provided engineering drawing and the accompanying CAD views, the vehicle is designed as a low-profile race car. Visually inspecting the side and rear views, the maximum height of the rear aerodynamic device (the tail section) is substantially lower than 1200 mm. Therefore, the design complies with this specific rule.
Answer:Yes
